title: Deployment Environments, OIDC, Workload Identity, and Cloud Authority · Delivery-System Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for deployment environments, oidc, workload identity, and cloud authority and requires the learner to defend the smallest safe environment and federated identity policy. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for environment and federated identity policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use named environments for approvals, branch and tag policies, scoped secrets, wait rules, URL evidence, and deployment history.
Request id-token write only in the deployment job and validate issuer, audience, subject, workflow ref, repository identity, event, and environment claims.
Exchange GitHub OIDC identity through a constrained Google Workload Identity Provider and service account without storing a key.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Ci Environment Protection Secret Scope · changed case · Quiz Ci Environment Protection Secret Scope
steps.instruction:
